title: Piquant Egyptian Bulgur Flatbread
description: A tangy, flaky flatbread made from mixed grains, perfect for midnight cravings. It is pure vegetarian, unleavened, and offers a bright, citrusy flavor punch.
instructions: Heat a heavy skillet over medium heat and lightly coat with a teaspoon of olive oil.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato paste, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper, cooking for 2 minutes to bloom the spices.
Pour in the bulgur and water, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and cover.
Simmer for 15 minutes until the bulgur absorbs the liquid and becomes tender.
Remove the skillet from heat and stir in the remaining olive oil and lemon juice, then let the dough cool for 5 minutes.
Transfer the dough onto a clean surface dusted with a little flour and divide it into 4 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a thin, flat disk, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place the flatbreads on the preheated skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side until they develop golden brown spots and puff slightly.
Remove from skillet, brush with a tiny amount of olive oil, and sprinkle with chopped parsley before serving.
Repeat with remaining dough portions, keeping cooked flatbreads warm in a low oven if desired.
